demographics.living_situation: lives in a supported group residential home with support workers

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Reported by his support worker) He's burning up, breathing very fast, and he's too weak to get out of bed or drink his fluids. He's just not himself.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He got worse very quickly over the last 12 hours — a sudden high fever and now he's breathing hard and barely responding, and I'm worried we can't manage this in the home tonight.

symptom_narrative.character: started with a high fever and shivering, then a wet cough bringing up yellow phlegm; now he is breathing fast and working hard to breathe and is very drowsy
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst I've seen him — normally chatty and independent, now too weak to sit up or drink
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: his chest and breathing; he seems congested and can't clear it
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started and steadily worsening
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far; paracetamol a few hours ago didn't seem to help much
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps getting worse on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: flushed and sweaty, coughing but too weak to bring the phlegm up, not eating or drinking
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: usually independent with daily activities in a supported setting; now bedbound and withdrawn

past_medical_history.how_managed: supported living, mild intellectual disability, usually verbal and interactive

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a hole in the heart that was fixed with an operation when he was little
past_medical_history.how_managed: surgically repaired; has a scar on his chest, no ongoing heart medications that staff are aware of

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: low muscle tone that makes his cough weak
past_medical_history.how_managed: baseline; staff sometimes help him clear his chest

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: paracetamol given about 4 hours ago for the fever

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: young adult in a supported group home; staff have moved him away from the other residents because of the fever and cough

objective_data_offered.value: 38.9 C on the group home thermometer
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: measured by support staff with a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: breathing about 28 times a minute when we counted
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: counted by a support worker, not a clinician

objective_data_offered.value: 92% on the home finger oximeter, on room air
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an unvalidated home fingertip oximeter

objective_data_offered.value: he looks flushed and sweaty, is using his neck and chest muscles to breathe, and is too weak and drowsy to sit himself up
